problem.title: Min Stack
problem.description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
assumptions: getMin() must return the minimum element in O(1).

example.input: push(-2), push(0), push(-3), getMin()→-3, pop(), top()→0, getMin()→-2
example.explanation: Maintain a second stack that tracks the minimum at each level. O(1) for all operations.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Stacks [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain a second stack that mirrors the main stack but only records a new value when it is less than or equal to the current minimum. On pop, if the removed value equals the top of the min-stack, pop the min-stack too. The key insight is that each position in the main stack has a corresponding minimum of everything at or below it, making getMin O(1) at all times.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Single Stack with Value-Min Pairs [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Instead of maintaining two separate stacks, store each element as a pair (value, current_min). On push, the current_min in the pair is min(val, stack.peek().min). getMin() simply returns the min from the top pair. All operations remain O(1) with no second stack needed.
